flow.accuracyHint: Estimate accuracy for closed tasks: plan = effort_days, actual = calendar duration (created→closed). ⚠ "spent" is wall-clock, not net effort. Real closures only (since Jun 12, states>1).
flow.blockedHint: Sprints that ever passed through BLOCKED status (by state history): {{ever}} of {{total}} ({{pct}}%). Currently blocked: {{nowBlocked}}.
flow.deployLagHint: Days from sprint closure (done_date) to the first release it shipped in (release_dates). Median/p75 across such sprints. On the right — how many done sprints still have no release.
flow.leadTimeHint: Days from the sprint's actual start (earliest valid_from in state history, sprint_starts slice) to closure (done_date). Median/p25/p75/max + histogram across closed sprints.
flow.releaseCadenceHint: Release pace per project: frequency = releases in the last 30 days ÷ (30/7) = releases per week. Median = median gap in days between the project's consecutive releases.
flow.releasePerWeekHint: Releases per week over the last 30 days
flow.throughputHint: Tasks closed per ISO week (KnowTaskHist → DONE). Counted since Jun 12 (earlier is bulk import) and only tasks with real status progression (states>1), excluding archival tasks "born done".

kpi.sprintsHint: Total sprints in LORE (totals.sprints). Below the value — how many are done.
kpi.tasksHint: Completion % = done tasks / all tasks across all sprints (tasks_done / tasks × 100).
kpi.releasesHint: Total releases (KnowRelease, all projects). current — those flagged is_current.
kpi.componentsHint: Total components. Below the value — how many have at least one linked sprint (coverage).
kpi.openSprintsHint: Open sprints for the current milestone: {{open}}. Total open in the system: {{total}}.

overview.tasksByStatusHint: Distribution of all tasks by status (status_raw classification). Segment length = share of status.
overview.sprintsByStatusHint: Distribution of all sprints by status (status_raw classification). Segment length = share of status.
overview.componentsHint: Components with sprints. Grouping: area (area field) · platform (root ancestor component: AIDA/SEIDR…) · project (component's dominant git project, derived via its sprints: Component←BELONGS_TO←Sprint→BELONGS_TO_PROJECT). Numbers: Sp = sprints, X/Y = tasks done/total, % = completion.
overview.releasesByProjectHint: Number of releases (KnowRelease) broken down by root git project.

planHealth.daysUntilHint: Calendar days from today to the current milestone's planned date (date_display).
planHealth.openSprintsHint: Open sprints for this milestone: {{open}}. Total open in the system: {{total}}.
planHealth.forecastHint: Forecast: milestone's open Sp ({{open}}) ÷ velocity ({{vel}} Sp/wk) = weeks to close.
planHealth.onTrackHint: Forecast vs. deadline: ~{{needed}} days needed for the remainder, {{days}} days to the milestone. {{detail}}

progress.velocityHint: Sprints closed per ISO week (by done_date). Bar = week, last one highlighted.
progress.avgVelocityHint: Average number of sprints closed per week over the shown period.
progress.velocityTrendHint: Trend: average velocity of the last 4 weeks vs. the previous 4, in %.
progress.velocityCVHint: Pace stability (coefficient of variation; lower = steadier)
progress.unreleasedBurnHint: Done for project = all sprints with a done_date. After M2 = done since Jun 3, not yet in a release. Open = status not done/cancelled.
progress.doneNotReleased: done sprints not yet in a release.
progress.allReleased: All done sprints are included in releases.
progress.m3Date: M3 (Jul 6) — planned date of the next release.
progress.burnupHint: Two cumulative lines by week: scope = sprints created by date (by valid_from); done = sprints closed by date (by done_date). Gap = backlog (scope creep). Respects the project filter.
progress.notEnoughData: Not enough data for this project.
progress.cumulativeHint: Running total of closed sprints by day (by done_date). Steepness = pace. Respects the project filter.
progress.paretoHint: Each component's share of all tasks (Pareto). Σ — cumulative share top to bottom. Coverage = components with sprints / all components.

qg.attentionHint: FAIL/WARN invariants across the whole fleet (content_md × latest ClRoutineMetric, status recomputed via compute_status — ADR-QG-002/004). Full detail — click a gate.
qg.qgDistributionHint: Distribution of Quality Gates by status (closed/active/deprecated). Large % = closed / (closed + active). Below — how many components have at least one QG.
qg.runHistoryHint: Runs are grouped by run_id (or routine+date). Time is taken from started_at/finished_at.
qg.statusGridHint: Color = worst status among the gate's invariants (content_md × latest metric, ADR-QG-002 compute_status). Gray = no runs/metrics.

mcpApi.lead: Direct AI-agent access (Claude Desktop / Claude Code / Cursor) to the LORE engine over the MCP protocol: read the plan / sprints / ADRs / decisions / releases and write statuses and tasks — without manual SQL. The server is a thin wrapper over backend :9100; all SQL-slice composition and whitelisting stays on the server.
mcpApi.toolsEmpty: Nothing found under the current filter.
mcpApi.toolsNote1: Write tools are sent with the header
mcpApi.toolsNote2: are versioned (SCD2 — history is never lost) and mutate the shared
mcpApi.toolsNote4: creates the full SCD2 structure: vertex + KnowADRHist (valid_to=null) + HAS_STATE edge — the ADR body is read specifically from the hist row.
mcpApi.toolsNote5: Partial (amend) calls on all upsert tools have been safe since 2026-07 — the SQL SET is built dynamically, an omitted field is left untouched (previously an omitted parameter was silently nulled out).
mcpApi.toolsNote6: the standard path is soft-delete via status (
mcpApi.toolsNote7: with status="cancelled"/"deprecated"/"archived" — history is preserved). A real hard-delete exists only for
mcpApi.toolsNote9: both explicitly marked as "for test artifacts only". The other types (Sprint/Task/Release/Component/Milestone/QualityGate/Runbook/Doc) deliberately have no hard-delete tool — real data is never deleted, only archived via status.
mcpApi.toolsNote10: The only one not yet implemented in the set is
mcpApi.toolsNote12: (the RAGVSDL showcase) — live on a separate "MCP API" page under "Research" (
mcpApi.sliceCatalogNote2: returns — a live whitelist of parameterized queries. Each slice is called via
mcpApi.backendDownRest: is not responding — the catalog is unavailable. Start the backend (see "Operations" below).
mcpApi.opsStep1: Check the backend (a common cause of "MCP not responding"):
mcpApi.opsStep2: If the backend is down — start it (Docker is recommended):
mcpApi.opsStep3: Build the MCP server (if not built yet):
mcpApi.opsStep4: The client launches the process over stdio itself — no need to "keep it alive" separately. After editing
mcpApi.smokeTestNote: Smoke test without a client (direct stdio JSON-RPC):
mcpApi.diag1a: Tools are listed, but every call errors
mcpApi.diag3b: ArcadeDB is unreachable or the password is wrong
mcpApi.diag4a: Server doesn't start under the client
mcpApi.clientOpenCodeNote: same MCP server, but each agent gets its own
mcpApi.clientOpenCodeNote2: this block IS the lazy mechanism on this client (there's no server-side dispatcher). Below is an example for
mcpApi.clientOpenCodeNote3: the other 6 profiles work the same way, just with their own allow-list (ready-made blocks live in
mcpApi.clientOpenCodeNote4: — each file is already a valid agent-config fragment, so it can be included by reference instead of copying).
mcpApi.gateText: Profiles are a visibility filter, not security: the server will still execute any call with
mcpApi.gateText2: The one real restriction lives on the backend —
mcpApi.gateText6: (ADR-LORE-014 §4). No one accepts their own work.
mcpApi.lazyClaude: ToolSearch — tool schemas load on demand, not all at once. Only names sit in context.
mcpApi.lazyIntro: 60 tools is too many to dump on every agent: context bloats and the role blurs ("anyone can write"). ADR-LORE-014 solves this
mcpApi.lazyIntro2: — the server always returns the full list, and narrowing happens client-side, by tool name.
mcpApi.lazyTitle: Lazy tool access and role profiles
mcpApi.lazyWhyNames: This is exactly why the renaming was done.
mcpApi.lazyWhyNames3: naming scheme isn't cosmetic — it gives a stable prefix the client filters on. With the old names (
mcpApi.lazyWhyNames4: ) everything shared the same prefix, so there was nothing to filter on.
mcpApi.metaToolsNote: Meta-tools (a tool that hands out other tools) were deliberately NOT built — that would have been Phase 2, and it wasn't needed: client-side filtering solved the problem without new server machinery.
mcpApi.profilesFull: — primary, everything allowed (Claude / backfill). The other 6 are subagent:
mcpApi.profilesRead3: — no one is denied reading the graph, only writing is restricted.
mcpApi.profilesRest: plus an allow-list of their own categories.
mcpApi.projectNewRbac2: registering a git project is a management/architecture decision, not an operational one.
mcpApi.toolsNoteRoles: Tasks carry author/executor/reviewer_agent (ADR-LORE-014 §4) — the one hard SDLC gate: reviewer_agent must differ from executor_agent, otherwise
mcpApi.toolsNoteRoles2: task→done is rejected (409 NO_SELF_ACCEPTANCE). Role values are an extensible dictionary
mcpApi.toolsNoteRoles3: (see Dictionary below) — the UI registers a new role in the dictionary on first entry.

methodology.title: Status calculation methodology
methodology.subtitle: ADR-QG-002 SMART-QG
methodology.gteLine: (higher=better): ≥ target → PASS · ≥ target×0.9 → WARN · else FAIL
methodology.lteLine: (lower=better): ≤ target → PASS · ≤ target×1.1 → WARN · else FAIL
methodology.overallLine: Overall: FAIL if ≥1 FAIL · WARN if any WARN/SKIP · all SKIP → SKIP · else PASS.

search.placeholder: Search all of LORE — morphology, bodies, prefix…
search.partial: Results are incomplete — search failed for types
search.bareWarn: any component filter will hide them
search.emptyHint: Clear facets or simplify the query
search.modeSmartHint: Each word goes in as (word OR word*) — morphology plus prefix
search.modeExactHint: Exact word forms only, no prefix
search.capped: hit the per-type cap — narrow the query
search.mathHint: BM25 (share of the best in its own type) × type priority
search.legendRankText: BM25 × type priority. The priority is ours, not the engine's.
search.legendBarText: rank relative to the top hit of this same query.
search.legendWhereText: worked out on our side: ArcadeDB does not report which field matched.
search.caveatTitleBoost: A title match does not outweigh a body match: title_boost had no effect in ArcadeDB.
search.caveatScores: Scores are comparable within one query only, never across queries.
search.caveatProjects: Project is not filled in everywhere: sprints and PRs have it, specs and tasks do not. Filtering by project will hide everything else.

admin.adminGives: Administration: people, dictionaries, settings, enabling auth
admin.agentsNote: AI agents are client roles of service accounts (the "agents" axis, agent_scope claim). Provisioned by script, not created by hand. Rotation shows the secret ONCE — LORE does not store it.
admin.byObjectNote: The reverse question — the one asked on an access review: who can touch this? Operations available only to people are highlighted in red (RBAC spec §4).
admin.canonConfirm: I understand: this is a canon dictionary, the change affects the whole corpus
admin.canonWarn: ⚠ Canon dictionary (ADR-LORE-010): these values are baked into statuses across the whole corpus. Editing label/color/icon is safe; codes cannot be edited here at all.
admin.cantEnableH: Auth cannot be enabled right now.
admin.cardHistoryNone: No history yet: an audit of admin operations will arrive with AL-20/AL-43 and land here. The screen does not show what the system does not yet record.
admin.chkAdmins: At least one person has the admin role
admin.chkAdminsN: Currently: {{n}}. Add yourself on the "People" tab and assign admin.
admin.chkAdminsUnknown: Number of admins unknown: the KC bridge isn't configured or didn't respond.
admin.chkScope: Agent permissions are checked on the backend
admin.chkScopeSub: AgentScopeFilter is not yet enabled (AL-17): once auth is on, agents will pass into any family.
admin.denialsEmpty: No denials since the last restart.
admin.denialsNA: Unavailable (admin role required, or backend is an old version).
admin.denialsSrc: Source: process memory since the last restart (ring buffer, AL-45). Long-term audit by axis is AL-20.
admin.devBannerB: Anyone who opens this address can administer LORE: the role comes from config, not a token. Development mode.
admin.enableAuthNote: The actual flip is an env var (LORE_AUTH_ENABLED=true) + restart per RUNBOOK-AUTH-OMILORE, all flags together (AL-12 — owner in person). This button becomes a working form once a runtime toggle exists.
admin.iconBad: no icon with this name — it will render empty.
admin.kcErrB: The request failed or didn't get through. The state of the records is unknown — this is not an empty list.
admin.kcForbiddenB: Access management is open only to the admin role. This is NOT an empty list: records may exist, they just aren't visible to you.
admin.kcForbiddenH: No permission to view this list
admin.kcOffB: The bridge to KC isn't configured, so there's nowhere to get the list from. This doesn't mean there are no records — their state is currently unknown. Setup: KC_ADMIN_CLIENT_SECRET in .env, see RUNBOOK-ADMIN-LORE. The rest of LORE works as usual.
admin.lastAdminB: Removing this role would leave LORE without an administrator — the backend will reject the operation (AL-35) until a second admin exists.
admin.newSecret: New secret for {{c}} — copy it now, it won't be shown again:
admin.noAgents: no one — forbidden to all agents, including agent-full
admin.noKnownSettings: The app doesn't read any app_setting key yet — the registry of known parameters is empty (AL-38). Once a real parameter exists, it gets a row here with type, default and description; free-form key entry is gone: no one will ever read a made-up key.
admin.noPassNote: The password is set in Keycloak (reset link/console) — LORE neither accepts nor stores it.
admin.notEnforcedB: The backend does not read the agent_scope claim: right now it's a tool-visibility filter, not enforcement. Turned on by the LORE_AUTH_ENABLED flag — its state is visible in "Settings".
admin.notEnforcedH: The permissions in this table are not enforced.
admin.notEnforcedUnkB: The backend did not respond to the auth preflight, so the enforcement state is unconfirmed. Treat this table as a statement of intent until the connection is restored.
admin.notEnforcedUnkH: Whether these permissions are enforced is currently unknown.
admin.noUsersB: Keycloak is responding fine, the realm just has no accounts. Add yourself first — BEFORE enabling authentication: it turns off config-based access, and there will be no one left to log in.
admin.noUsersH: No one has been added yet
admin.pfLoading: Checks are loading (or the KC bridge is unavailable — the checklist will show that explicitly once it responds).
admin.preview: This is how it will look
admin.rolesNote2: Two axes that never mix: people sign in with a password and carry realm roles (seer_roles), agents sign in with a secret and carry agent_scope. Agent scope is edited in mcp-server/agent-profiles/*.json (D6, read-only here).
admin.setProj: session default project of the MCP process (env, ADR-LORE-017) — set in .mcp.json/OpenCode config, not read from the UI
admin.tagsNote: Read-only (D6): merge/rename is iteration 2 (TAGGED_WITH edge migration, AL-29 awaits an owner decision).
admin.unknownB: These keys live in the database (dict_type=app_setting), but the app doesn't read them — most likely leftovers from a manual edit. Deleting them changes nothing (soft delete: is_active=false).
admin.usageNA: usage count for this dictionary arrives with AL-30/SV-10
admin.usageWarn2: Changing the color/icon will change how they look across all of LORE; deactivating requires confirmation.
admin.usersNote: People are Keycloak realm roles (the "people" axis). LORE stores no passwords: the user sets it in KC. The super-admin role is assigned only in the KC console (outside the bridge, D11).
admin.viewerGives: Read-only access to LORE. Does not allow into administration

integrations.title: connectors for collecting metrics and publishing. Tokens — by secret reference, not by value.

integrationEditor.errSecretRef: secret_ref must be a reference: "env:X" / "vault:X" / "oauth:X" / "secret:X" — not the token value
integrationEditor.secretHintOk: ⚠️ Never paste the token itself — only a reference like env:X / vault:X / oauth:X / secret:X
integrationEditor.secretHintWarn: ✕ Looks like a value, not a reference. Format: env:X / vault:X / oauth:X / secret:X

plan.desc: 2-month content push · VC + Habr + Telegram · social-capital stage.

publicationEditor.annotationPlaceholder: persistent meta: master source, override rule, release context…

publications.desc: main text + channel variants: one publication groups its versions; each has its own text, status, and image.

rubricManager.emptyHint: no categories yet — you can add one from the publication/keyword form

overview.desc: plan, publications, and social capital analytics — in one place. Links to tasks and releases in Forseti.
